game_info.title: Go Escape!
game_info.concept: **Go Escape!**: Go Escape! is a physics-based platformer where players guide a ball through a series of increasingly complex levels by tapping the screen to initiate a jump of fixed height and distance. The ball automatically rolls forward along a predetermined path, and players must precisely time taps to jump over gaps between platforms, avoid static and moving obstacles, and land safely on subsequent platforms. Levels consist of 5-10 distinct platform segments, each introducing new challenges like vanishing platforms that disappear 0.5 seconds after contact or moving blocks that traverse the path every 2 seconds. Successfully navigating a level unlocks the next, with difficulty scaling through faster obstacle movements, narrower platforms, and more intricate jump sequences. The game features a single life per level; colliding with an obstacle or falling off a platform results in immediate failure and a restart of the current level.
game_info.description: Guide a rolling ball through challenging platforming levels! Your ball automatically rolls forward, and you must time your jumps perfectly to clear gaps, avoid obstacles, and reach the goal. Watch out for vanishing platforms that disappear after you land on them, and moving blocks that can knock you off course. One mistake means starting the level over. Complete all levels to win!
game_info.controls:
SPACE: Jump (fixed height and distance)
ENTER: Start game from menu
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart to main menu from game over screen

TEST_1.test_description: Testing basic movement and jump mechanics. Validates that the ball rolls forward automatically at the correct speed and that the jump input produces consistent jump height and distance. Also tests that the player can successfully land on platforms after jumping.
TEST_1.strategy_description: Jump at regular intervals (every 60 frames) to test jump consistency. The ball should maintain forward momentum while jumping and land safely on platforms. This tests the core physics integration between Matter.js and the game logic.
TEST_1.expected_outcome: The ball should jump consistently with each input, maintaining forward velocity while in the air. Jumps should have predictable arcs allowing the ball to clear small gaps. The test is successful if the ball completes at least the first platform segment without falling.

TEST_2.test_description: Testing full level completion and win condition. This test attempts to complete all levels by executing a pre-programmed sequence of jumps timed to clear all obstacles and gaps. Validates level progression, goal detection, and win state.
TEST_2.strategy_description: Execute a carefully timed sequence of jumps at specific frame counts designed to clear all obstacles in all levels. The timing is based on the ball's constant forward velocity and the known positions of gaps and obstacles. Jumps are triggered at frames: 120, 240, 360, 480, 600, 750, 900, 1050.
TEST_2.expected_outcome: The ball should successfully navigate through all platforms, avoid all obstacles, and reach the goal in each level. The game should transition from level to level automatically, and finally reach the GAME_OVER_WIN state. Success means completing all 3 levels without falling or hitting obstacles.

TEST_3.test_description: Testing collision detection and game over condition. Validates that the ball properly detects when it falls below the screen or collides with obstacles, triggering the appropriate game over state.
TEST_3.strategy_description: Avoid jumping entirely, allowing the ball to fall into the first gap. This tests the fall detection system and ensures the game properly transitions to GAME_OVER_LOSE when the player fails.
TEST_3.expected_outcome: The ball should roll forward without jumping, fall into the first gap when it reaches it, and trigger the GAME_OVER_LOSE state. The test is successful when the game phase changes to GAME_OVER_LOSE and displays the appropriate failure message.

TEST_4.test_description: Testing vanishing platform mechanic. Validates that platforms disappear after the specified time delay and that the ball falls through disappeared platforms. This tests the dynamic platform behavior.
TEST_4.expected_outcome: Vanishing platforms should visually indicate their temporary nature, disappear exactly 0.5 seconds after the ball lands on them, and the ball should fall through the disappeared platform. Success is when the vanishing mechanic triggers consistently.
TEST_4.description: Testing vanishing platform mechanic. Validates that platforms disappear after the specified time delay and that the ball falls through disappeared platforms. This tests the dynamic platform behavior.
TEST_4.expected: Vanishing platforms should visually indicate their temporary nature, disappear exactly 0.5 seconds after the ball lands on them, and the ball should fall through the disappeared platform. Success is when the vanishing mechanic triggers consistently.

TEST_5.test_description: Testing moving obstacle collision. Validates that moving blocks follow their paths correctly and that collisions with these obstacles result in game over or knockback effects.
TEST_5.strategy_description: Time jumps to intentionally collide with a moving obstacle. This tests whether the collision detection works correctly with moving bodies and whether the game responds appropriately to obstacle hits.
TEST_5.expected_outcome: Moving obstacles should traverse their paths smoothly. When the ball collides with a moving obstacle, it should either trigger game over or receive significant knockback. The test succeeds when obstacle collision is properly detected and handled.

TEST_6.test_description: Testing level progression and difficulty scaling. Validates that completing a level unlocks the next level and that subsequent levels have increased difficulty with faster obstacles, narrower platforms, or more complex jump sequences.
TEST_6.strategy_description: Complete the first level successfully by making all necessary jumps, then observe the transition to level 2. Compare the difficulty elements between levels (platform widths, obstacle speeds, gap sizes).
TEST_6.expected_outcome: Successfully reaching the goal should trigger level completion and load the next level. Level 2 should be noticeably more difficult than level 1 with at least one of: narrower platforms, faster moving obstacles, or more frequent gaps. Success is automatic progression through levels.

TEST_7.test_description: Testing pause and restart functionality. Validates that the ESC key properly pauses and unpauses the game, freezing all physics and gameplay, and that the R key correctly restarts from game over states.
TEST_7.strategy_description: Press ESC during gameplay to pause, verify physics stops, then press ESC again to resume. After a game over, press R to return to the start screen, then press ENTER to start a new game.
TEST_7.expected_outcome: ESC should toggle between PLAYING and PAUSED states, freezing all ball movement and physics when paused. R from game over should return to START screen with the game properly reset. All state transitions should be clean without errors.
